coaching.coaching: Plan how verification steps for each acceptance criterion will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification steps for each acceptance criterion

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: improve
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tag the post-merge Gerrit Verified evidence in AC2 and AC5 with `[operator-attested]` (per ADR 0043) and add a concrete attestation record placeholder (e.g. 'Verified +1 on change <ID> post-merge, timestamp <T>') so the proving evidence is formally captured on the ticket rather than implicitly 'watched'.
evidence: 'the Verified vote still works end-to-end (confirmed by the first post-merge run + the next change's Verified +1)'
'lands through Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1'

'g2p dispatches gerrit-verify.yaml from main's ref, so THIS change's own Verified run uses the OLD inline gate; the refactored reusable path first runs post-merge via test.yml (branch-head), watched immediately after landing'
Plan lacks an [operator-attested] tag on the post-merge portions of AC2 and AC5, which are the only ACs that exercise the cutover path end-to-end in production.
E6(f): cutover to new code path needs an AC that exercises it end-to-end as in production — green offline tests alone are not sufficient.
findings.finding: AC2 and AC5 both include post-merge Gerrit Verified +1 outcomes ('confirmed by the first post-merge run + the next change's Verified +1'; 'lands through Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1') as acceptance evidence for the critical cutover to the reusable workflow path. These are not in-session provable and are not tagged [operator-attested]. Per E6(f), a cutover to a new production code path requires an AC that exercises it end-to-end as in production; the plan explicitly acknowledges this can only be verified post-merge. Without the [operator-attested] tag, the rubric cannot treat a post-merge external vote as a valid in-session proving command, leaving the most critical AC (does the reusable path actually work for Verified?) unverifiable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('the Verified vote still works end-to-end (confirmed by the first post-merge run + the next change's Verified +1)') and AC5 ('lands through Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1'); Validation note
scenarios: Agent marks the plan done after local `make lint` and workflow tests pass, but the reusable delegation is subtly mis-wired (e.g. wrong job name, missing needs edge) and the Verified vote silently breaks post-merge — the gap between in-session green and post-merge production is exactly the risk E6(f) guards against.
A reviewer auditing completeness cannot verify whether the Gerrit Verified +1 was actually observed for the reusable path without an attestation record on the ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, prepend '[operator-attested]' to the clause: '[operator-attested] The Verified vote still works end-to-end — record the first post-merge run's conclusion URL and the next change's Verified +1 change-id here.' In AC5, split into two sub-bullets: one in-session ('make lint + workflow tests pass — provable by running the test suite pre-merge') and one operator-attested ('[operator-attested] lands through Gerrit with Verified +1 — record change-id and vote timestamp here').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verifiable condition to AC2 (or a new AC) asserting that the old inline lint/pip-audit/test jobs are ABSENT from test.yml and the old monolithic build-and-test job body is ABSENT from gerrit-verify.yaml after the rewire.
evidence: AC2 text: 'test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml BOTH delegate to ./.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml; gerrit-verify's reusable-call job is named build-and-test and vote still needs it'
No AC line says 'inline lint/pip-audit/test jobs are deleted from test.yml' or 'monolithic build-and-test job is removed from gerrit-verify.yaml'
What section says 'delete its inline lint/pip-audit/test jobs' — described work with no covering criterion
findings.finding: AC2 verifies that delegation strings exist and that the vote still needs 'build-and-test', but does NOT verify that the OLD inline jobs are removed from test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml. An implementation that adds the reusable call while leaving the inline jobs intact would pass AC2 yet defeat the entire consolidation goal.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / 'Rewire callers' in What
scenarios: Implementer adds reusable call but forgets to delete inline jobs; duplicate execution goes undetected; parity test may also miss it since it now checks delegation strings, not absence of inline definitions.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2: '…; test.yml contains no inline lint, pip-audit, or test job definitions; gerrit-verify.yaml contains no inline build-and-test job steps (only the reusable-call job with that name).'  Optionally add a companion assertion to the rewritten test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate that fails if the delegation string exists AND an inline duplicate is also present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan to the Validation note (e.g., a revert commit procedure, a named fallback branch strategy, or an expand-contract sequence that keeps inline jobs active until the reusable is confirmed green) so implementers know exactly how to restore the Verified gate if post-merge validation fails.
evidence: 'g2p dispatches gerrit-verify.yaml from main's ref, so THIS change's own Verified run uses the OLD inline gate; the refactored reusable path first runs post-merge via test.yml (branch-head), watched immediately after landing.'
'actionlint (make lint) validates the reusable uses: wiring + workflow_call input schema locally pre-merge'
No section describes a rollback commit, feature-flag, or expand-contract sequence if post-merge Verified breaks.
T4 rubric: 'acknowledgement alone is insufficient; require the reversibility mechanism'
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive, largely irreversible structural change to two CI entry-point files (deleting inline jobs from test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml) but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan for the post-merge window. The Validation note acknowledges the bootstrap risk but only names pre-merge validators (actionlint, rewritten parity tests); it does not describe what to do if the reusable wiring is silently broken post-merge and the Verified vote stops working.
findings.location: Validation note / Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / What 'Rewire callers'
scenarios: Reusable file has a subtle YAML error that actionlint misses; post-merge gerrit-verify.yaml's reusable-call job silently fails or is skipped; the next submitted change gets no Verified vote and the team must diagnose under pressure with no stated recovery path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Validation note: 'Back-out: if test.yml's first post-merge run or the next change's Verified run fails, revert this commit (git revert <sha>) to restore the inline jobs; the reusable file can be debugged on a branch before re-landing. No data or schema migration is involved, so revert is clean.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief entry to AGENTS.md (or equivalent design doc) documenting the reusable-workflow convention: the `_*.yml` naming pattern, the 'no secrets' contract, the checkout parameterization interface, and a pointer to `_optionality.yml` and `_build-and-test.yml` as canonical examples.
evidence: Plan establishes a new convention ('the pattern _optionality.yml already proves') but treats it as implicit, with no doc update specified
AC items cover test file updates and workflow wiring but no AGENTS.md or ADR entry
T5e rubric: 'A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / AGENTS.md / design doc update'
T7 rubric: 'A new pattern/contract/config/CLI gets a doc/ADR'
Absence: no mention of documenting the reusable-workflow pattern or the 'no secrets' contract for future reusable additions
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new reusable-workflow architectural pattern (_build-and-test.yml as a second workflow_call reusable alongside _optionality.yml), establishing a project convention for CI decomposition, but specifies no update to AGENTS.md, a design doc, or an ADR capturing this convention for future contributors.
scenarios: A future contributor adds a third workflow and does not know to follow the reusable pattern, re-introducing inline duplication
The 'no secrets' contract for reusables is violated in a follow-up (e.g., golden-path extraction) because the constraint is undocumented
findings.suggested_fix: In AGENTS.md under a CI/Workflows section, add: 'Shared gate logic lives in `.github/workflows/_<name>.yml` reusable (workflow_call) files. These take no secrets and parameterize checkout via gerrit-refspec/gerrit-project/gerrit-url inputs (empty = branch-head). New shared gate logic must go into a reusable rather than being inlined per-caller. See `_optionality.yml` and `_build-and-test.yml` as canonical examples.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly that tests 4 and 5 replace BOTH `("test.yml", _TEST_YML)` and `("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)` loop entries with `("_build-and-test.yml", _BUILD_AND_TEST_YML)` (single entry), since only the reusable owns the pre-commit step post-refactor — and add a `_BUILD_AND_TEST_YML` path constant to the file.
evidence: test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci iterates `for label, path in (("test.yml", _TEST_YML), ("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)):` with `assert steps, f"{label}: no pre-commit run --all-files step found"` (lines 199–204)
test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit has identical structure with `assert steps, f"{label}: no pre-commit run --all-files step found"` (lines 228–233)

Plan says only 'retarget _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml (the reusable now owns the single pre-commit step)' — no instruction to remove or replace the test.yml/gerrit-verify.yaml loop entries
After the refactor, test.yml's inline lint/pre-commit/test jobs are deleted, so _precommit_all_files_steps(test.yml_text) returns [] and `assert steps` fires before any hook assertion is reached
findings.finding: Tests 4 and 5 of test_ci_workflow_parity.py iterate over the HARDCODED pair `("test.yml", _TEST_YML), ("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)` with hard `assert steps` guards that abort when no `pre-commit run --all-files` step is found (lines 199–204 and 231–233). After the refactor both files will have their inline jobs removed, so `_precommit_all_files_steps` will return `[]` for both and the tests will error — not pass — before even reaching the skip/hook assertions. The plan says 'retarget _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml' but never specifies whether the existing `(test.yml, gerrit-verify.yaml)` loop entries are replaced entirely, reduced to just the reusable, or supplemented. This is a codebase-grounded ambiguity: the rewrite instruction is under-specified for the actual loop structure in the file.
findings.location: Plan section 'Correct premise: test_ci_workflow_parity.py enforces FIVE functions' — items 4 and 5; tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 191–248
scenarios: Developer lands the refactor, runs `make lint` / pytest — tests 4 and 5 both error with 'no pre-commit run --all-files step found' for test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, blocking CI even though the reusable correctly has the pre-commit step.
Developer only replaces the gerrit-verify.yaml path but keeps test.yml in the loop — test.yml still errors; the guard is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `_BUILD_AND_TEST_YML = _ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "_build-and-test.yml"` to the file-path constants, then change the loop in both tests from `for label, path in (("test.yml", _TEST_YML), ("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)):` to `for label, path in (("_build-and-test.yml", _BUILD_AND_TEST_YML),):`. The plan section should state this explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the plan that tests 4 and 5 must REPLACE the `for label, path in (('test.yml', _TEST_YML), ('gerrit-verify.yaml', _GERRIT_YML)):` loop with a single-target scan of `_build-and-test.yml`, AND add a new module-level `_BUILD_AND_TEST_YML` path constant alongside `_TEST_YML` and `_GERRIT_YML`.
evidence: Plan: 'retarget _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml (the reusable now owns the single pre-commit step)'
test_ci_workflow_parity.py line 199: `for label, path in (("test.yml", _TEST_YML), ("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)):`
test_ci_workflow_parity.py line 201: `assert steps, (f"{label}: no 'pre-commit run --all-files' step found ...")`

After the refactor, test.yml's lint job is deleted (replaced by delegation) — _precommit_all_files_steps(test.yml) returns [] because there is no `pre-commit run --all-files` step in test.yml anymore, causing assert steps to fire
Same issue for gerrit-verify.yaml: build-and-test becomes a `uses:` call with no steps array
findings.finding: The plan says 'retarget _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml' for test functions 4 and 5, but does not specify that the existing two-file loop tuple `(('test.yml', _TEST_YML), ('gerrit-verify.yaml', _GERRIT_YML))` must be REPLACED (not extended). After the refactor, both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml lose their inline pre-commit steps, so scanning either one returns `[]`, triggering the `assert steps` guard. The spec is underspecified: without explicit replacement, an implementer could add _build-and-test.yml as a third loop entry while leaving the two broken entries.
findings.location: ## Correct premise: test_ci_workflow_parity.py enforces FIVE functions, not one — items 4 and 5
scenarios: Implementer adds _build-and-test.yml as a third scan target but retains test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml in the loop → both assert-steps guards fail immediately on the updated test file
Implementer removes test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml from the loop but forgets to add _BUILD_AND_TEST_YML constant → NameError at import
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan under the function 4/5 retarget description: 'Add `_BUILD_AND_TEST_YML = _ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "_build-and-test.yml"` as a module-level constant, and replace the two-element loop `for label, path in (("test.yml", _TEST_YML), ("gerrit-verify.yaml", _GERRIT_YML)):` with `for label, path in (("_build-and-test.yml", _BUILD_AND_TEST_YML),):` in both functions 4 and 5.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that the test 2 rewrite must fully replace the two-variable diff logic (`scripts_in_test - scripts_in_gerrit`) with a single assertion: every `scripts/*.py` found in `_build-and-test.yml` is sufficient, and the gerrit-verify.yaml scan variable is removed entirely.
evidence: Plan: 'REWRITE: scan _build-and-test.yml for scripts/*.py (they now gate both lanes by construction); _INTENTIONAL_SCRIPT_ASYMMETRIES stays empty. The old test.yml-minus-gerrit diff is obsolete under a single source.'
test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 99-108: current test scans both test_yml and gerrit_yml for scripts/, computes `missing = scripts_in_test - scripts_in_gerrit`

After refactor: gerrit-verify.yaml will have no `scripts/*.py` references (it delegates). If the function retains its two-file structure, scripts_in_gerrit=={} → missing == all scripts in _build-and-test.yml → test always fails
findings.finding: The plan's rewrite of test 2 (test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci) says to scan `_build-and-test.yml` for `scripts/*.py`, but the existing test also checks `gerrit-verify.yaml` for the same scripts. After the refactor gerrit-verify.yaml no longer contains any `scripts/*.py` references inline (it delegates via `uses:`). The plan does not specify whether the gerrit-verify.yaml scan is dropped entirely or retained. If the old `scripts_in_gerrit` scan is kept, it will find zero matches, making `missing = scripts_in_test - scripts_in_gerrit - _INTENTIONAL_SCRIPT_ASYMMETRIES` effectively `missing = scripts_in_test` (all scripts appear missing), breaking every build.
findings.location: ## Correct premise: test_ci_workflow_parity.py enforces FIVE functions, not one — item 2
scenarios: Implementer reuses the existing function structure and only changes the scanned file for scripts_in_test, not scripts_in_gerrit → massive false-failure on every run
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'The rewritten test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci should scan ONLY _build-and-test.yml for scripts/*.py and assert the set is non-empty (or matches expected), eliminating the two-file diff; the `scripts_in_gerrit` variable and the subtraction logic are removed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the plan's positive rationale explaining why composite actions are structurally ruled out for _build-and-test.yml: a `./.github/actions/…` composite action requires the repo to be pre-checked-out, making the conditional branch-head vs. Gerrit-patchset checkout impossible inside the action — `workflow_call` is the only mechanism that supports parameterized checkout with no-secrets inheritance.
evidence: Plan states the approach is 'EXACTLY like _optionality.yml' without carrying forward why composite action is structurally ruled out.
_optionality.yml header comment: 'A LOCAL composite action cannot do this — a `./.github/actions/...` reference needs the repo already checked out — so the conditional checkout is inlined per job.'
Plan's positive rationale focuses on 'drift is impossible by construction' (the outcome) but not on the mechanism-selection reason that rules out the alternative structural approach.
findings.finding: The plan states the workflow_call reusable approach is correct but does not include a positive rationale for why the structurally different alternative (a composite action) is ruled out for _build-and-test.yml. The reason is load-bearing and non-obvious: a local composite action (./.github/actions/...) requires the repo to already be checked out, making the parameterized conditional checkout (branch-head vs. Gerrit patchset) impossible inside a composite action — so workflow_call is the only mechanism that achieves both the parameterized checkout and the no-secrets constraint. This reasoning exists in _optionality.yml's header comment but is absent from the plan.
findings.location: ## What — first paragraph; ## Why — rationale section
scenarios: An implementer reviewing the plan could question whether a composite action would be simpler and not find the rejection rationale, leading to a refactor attempt that discovers the checkout limitation only at implementation time.
A future reviewer of the plan cannot evaluate whether the workflow_call choice was considered against alternatives without reading _optionality.yml's comments.
findings.suggested_fix: In the '## What' section, after '(the pattern _optionality.yml already proves)', add: 'A composite action cannot be used here for the same reason as _optionality.yml: a local ./.github/actions/… reference requires the repo to already be checked out, making the conditional checkout (branch-head vs. Gerrit patchset) impossible inside a composite action.'

findings.finding: AC2 ('Verified vote still works end-to-end') has no proving command or step describing HOW to trigger the real gerrit-verify dispatch and what observable artifact to inspect to confirm Verified +1 was cast.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'The Verified vote still works end-to-end (a real gerrit-verify dispatch casts Verified +1).'

findings.finding: The plan asserts test_ci_workflow_parity.py 'exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync', but a direct read of the file reveals it also enforces three independent behavioral invariants: (a) pre-commit branch-sensitive hook skipping in both workflows (test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci), (b) CI-redundant hook de-duplication skipping (test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit), and (c) optionality reusable-workflow wiring with GERRIT_REFSPEC (test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path). These guards are not merely 'copy-sync policing' — they enforce safety properties that would be silently lost if the file is naively deleted under the 'solely to police sync' premise.
findings.location: Why section: 'tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync'; AC line 3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's Why or AC, replace 'exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync' with a note that the file contains five test functions, three of which (pre-commit SKIP parity × 2, optionality wiring × 1) enforce behavioral invariants independent of copy-sync and must be explicitly re-expressed — e.g. as assertions on the new reusable workflow's interface — rather than retired.

findings.finding: test_ci_workflow_parity.py hard-codes scans of gerrit-verify.yaml's raw text for all gate command signatures (_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES, scripts regex, optionality wiring, pre-commit SKIP checks). After build-and-test is extracted into _build-and-test.yml, every assertion that reads _GERRIT_YML for those command strings will fail (the commands will be in the reusable file, not inline). The plan says 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what the updated test should verify nor which file(s) it should scan.
findings.location: AC line 3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently'; tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 81, 88, 100, 103, 118, 199, 228
findings.suggested_fix: Update _GERRIT_YML assertions to verify that gerrit-verify.yaml *calls* ./.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml (structural wiring check), and redirect the gate-command-signature assertions (_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES, scripts regex, pre-commit SKIP) to scan _build-and-test.yml instead of _GERRIT_YML.

findings.finding: The plan analogises the proposed `_build-and-test.yml` reusable workflow directly to `_optionality.yml`, but the two cases differ in a load-bearing way: `_optionality.yml` has no upstream `needs:` dependencies in either caller, whereas `gerrit-verify.yaml`'s `build-and-test` job sits behind `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]`. A `workflow_call` job cannot inherit `needs:` from inside the callee — the Gerrit caller must wrap the reusable call in a job that declares those `needs:`. The plan does not acknowledge this wrapper requirement, so an implementer following it naively will either (a) lose the `clear-vote`/`require-ticket` ordering guarantee or (b) discover the structural complexity mid-implementation. This is premature abstraction if the structural fit is not confirmed before work starts.
findings.location: Why section / AC line 1 ('invoked by both test.yml (branch head) and gerrit-verify.yaml (patchset)')

findings.finding: The plan describes parameterizing checkout by gerrit-refspec ('branch-head for the mirror lane, exact patchset for the Verified gate') as a key deliverable, but no AC explicitly verifies that this parameterization is correct for both invocation paths.
findings.location: Why section ('checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec') vs. AC1 ('invoked by both test.yml … and gerrit-verify.yaml')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to: 'The Verified vote still works end-to-end, including confirmation that gerrit-verify.yaml passes the patchset refspec (not branch head) to the reusable workflow, verified by inspecting the workflow_call inputs in a real dispatch log.'

findings.finding: AC3 says test_ci_workflow_parity.py will be 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what new structural coverage (if any) validates that both callers wire the reusable workflow's parameters correctly — in particular that gerrit-refspec is threaded through properly in each invocation. If retired without replacement, parameter-wiring bugs (e.g. wrong ref passed to _build-and-test.yml) would only be caught by the live Verified-vote dispatch, not by any fast-feedback unit/structural test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'If test_ci_workflow_parity.py is retired, add a replacement structural test (e.g. assert both callers pass `gerrit-refspec` and that the value differs between test.yml branch-head and gerrit-verify.yaml patchset invocations) to preserve fast-feedback coverage of parameter wiring.'

findings.finding: AC3 says test_ci_workflow_parity.py will be 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what structural invariant replaces its safety net. The parity test currently auto-detects new gates added to test.yml that are missing from gerrit-verify.yaml — a historically-triggered protection. After consolidation, gates inside the reusable file are automatically shared, but gates outside it (golden-path, lint, pip-audit, require-ticket, verify-identity if they remain caller-side) still need drift protection, and new risks emerge (e.g. a caller omitting a required `with:` input, matrix mismatch). Retiring the test without specifying the replacement leaves the system with a net reduction in observable correctness enforcement.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
